title:
Lesson 1:
At the doctor
description: Medical vocabulary: hasta (sick), ağrı (pain), ilaç (medicine), doktor (doctor), hastane (hospital).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I don't feel well.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: What hurts?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: My stomach hurts.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: You should go to the doctor.

title:
Lesson 2:
Feeling words
description: Emotions: yorgun (tired), mutlu (happy), üzgün (sad), kızgın (angry), sinirli (nervous).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How do you feel?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I am very happy today.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I am a little tired.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Why are you tired?

title:
Lesson 3:
Describing things
description: Adjectives for things: büyük (big), küçük (small), yeni (new), eski (old), güzel (pretty), çirkin (ugly).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Your house is very big.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, it's big and new.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: My apartment is small.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: But it's pretty.

title:
Lesson 4:
Expressing necessity
description: '-meli/-malı' = have to/must. 'Gitmeliyim' = I have to go. Expressing obligations.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you have to do today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I have to work.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I have to study.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I need to finish a project.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Is it going to rain tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm not sure. Maybe.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you going to the party?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Probably yes.

title:
Lesson 6:
Review and celebration
description: Congratulations on reaching lesson 73! Review key concepts: greetings, verbs, time, daily life, past tense. Keep practicing!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We did it! We finished seventy-three lessons.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Evet, artık daha fazla Türkçe konuşuyorum.
dialogue.english: Yes, now I speak more Turkish.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We learned a lot.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: It's true. I am very happy.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Congratulations! Keep practicing!

title:
Lesson 7:
I Love It / I Like It
description: 'Bayılıyorum' = I love it / I'm crazy about it (stronger). 'Seviyorum' = I love/like it. 'Çok seviyorum' = I like it a lot. 'Hoşuma gidiyor' = I like it (it pleases me).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you try the new restaurant?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I love it! The food is amazing.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you like the dessert too?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Çok sevdim. Bu hafta tekrar gideceğim.
dialogue.english: I liked it a lot. I'm going back this week.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Ben de gelmek istiyorum! Yeni yerler denemek hoşuma gidiyor.
dialogue.english: I want to come too! I like trying new places.

vocabulary.english: I love it / I'm crazy about it
I like it / I love it
I like it / it pleases me

title:
Lesson 8:
It Bothers Me / My Mood
description: 'Rahatsız ediyor beni' = It bothers me. 'Çok rahatsız ediyor' = It really bothers me. 'Keyfim yerinde' = I'm in a good mood. 'Keyifim yok' or 'Moralim bozuk' = I'm in a bad mood.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you okay? You look bothered.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Moralim bozuk. Gürültü çok rahatsız ediyor beni.
dialogue.english: I'm in a bad mood. The noise really bothers me.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I understand. Can I do anything?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Hayır, olsun. Yarın keyfim yerinde olur.
dialogue.english: No, it's okay. Tomorrow I'll be in a good mood.

title:
Lesson 9:
Fun / Boring
description: 'Eğlenceli' = It's fun / entertaining. 'Sıkıcı' = It's boring. These adjectives describe experiences and places. 'Sıkıldım' = I got bored (personal feeling).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How was the party?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Çok eğlenceliydi. Bütün gece dans ettik.
dialogue.english: It was really fun. We danced all night.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Was the movie fun too?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: No, it was very boring. I fell asleep.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Ha! It seemed boring to me too.

title:
Lesson 10:
I'm Distracted / I Need to Focus
description: 'Dalgınım' = I'm distracted / spaced out. 'Odaklanmam gerekiyor' = I need to focus. 'Dikkat etmem gerekiyor' = I need to pay attention. 'Başka bir şey düşünüyordum' = I was thinking about something else.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Seni üç kez çağırdım. Duymadın mı?
dialogue.english: I called you three times. Didn't you hear?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Özür dilerim, başka bir şey düşünüyordum.
dialogue.english: Sorry, I was thinking about something else.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: You're always distracted lately.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: You're right. I need to focus more.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Especially at work. You need to pay attention.

vocabulary.english: I was thinking about something else
